message:
BREAKING: preserve host paths in containers instead of /workspace

Changes container mounting to preserve exact host directory paths inside containers:

Before:
- Host: /Users/jesse/Documents/GitHub/myproject
- Container: /workspace (mounted from host path)
- Working dir: /workspace

After:
- Host: /Users/jesse/Documents/GitHub/myproject
- Container: /Users/jesse/Documents/GitHub/myproject (same path)
- Working dir: /Users/jesse/Documents/GitHub/myproject

Benefits:
- Absolute paths work identically between host and container
- Git worktree references maintain correct paths
- Tool configurations with hardcoded paths work consistently
- Symlinks preserve correct relative relationships
- Cross-container path consistency for multi-container workflows

Implementation:
- Mount worktree at host path instead of /workspace
- Set working directory to host path
- Create parent directory structure in container as needed
- Update all exec commands to use host paths
- Add comprehensive TDD test coverage

This is a breaking change - existing scripts expecting /workspace will need updates.

🤖 Generated with Claude Code

files.patch:
@@ -260,7 +260,7 @@ On first run, packnplay prompts you to choose which credentials to enable by def
 
 - `~/.claude` → mounted read-write (skills, plugins, history)
 - `~/.claude.json` → copied into container (avoids file lock conflicts)
-- Worktree → mounted at `/workspace`
+- Worktree → mounted at same host path (preserves absolute paths)
 - Main repo `.git` → mounted at its real path (git commands work)
 
 ### Environment Variables
